numbers_only.response: The Moon's gravity pulls on Earth, causing the oceans to bulge outward in the direction of the Moon. This results in high tide. As Earth rotates, different parts experience these bulges, creating two high tides and two low tides each day. Sunlight also affects tides but less strongly than lunar gravity.

802.11 standard defines protocols for wireless local area networks (WLANs). Devices connect to a router that broadcasts a signal, often using radio waves at specific frequencies. The router and connected devices communicate via these signals, exchanging data packets.

Once upon a time, in a faraway land, there was a mischievous fox who loved to steal chickens from the nearby farm. The farmer tried various tricks to catch the fox but none worked until he thought of a clever plan. He laid out a large pile of chicken feathers and told everyone that he had finally caught the fox. Curious, the fox came to inspect the supposed catch and stepped right into the trap. From then on, the fox learned a valuable lesson about being too clever by half.

Once upon a time, in a faraway land, there was a wise old owl who lived in a great forest. The owl had lived for many years and knew all about the creatures that lived in the woods. One day, a young rabbit came to the owl with a problem. The rabbit had lost his tail, and he was worried that the other animals would think less of him because of it. The owl listened carefully and then said, “My dear rabbit, tails are not what make you special. Once upon a time, there was a cat who loved chasing laser pointers. One day, the cat’s owner got a new job that required them to travel frequently. The owner tried to leave the cat alone with a pet sitter, but the cat was so distressed by being left home that they refused to eat or play. So the owner decided to bring the laser pointer along on their trips and spend a few minutes playing with the cat each night before bed. This helped the cat feel less lonely and made it much easier to take care of the cat while traveling.
